rationale.claude: This is the 'dump everything' baseline — a resume that has not been tailored at all. It is what comes out when the system fails to choose what to leave behind. The reader cannot tell what role this person is targeting. Skills run 50+ items mixing combat-medic terms (CBRN, Hazmat) with help-desk terms (RustDesk, Microsoft 365) into one untriaged blob. Date format is raw YYYY-MM, not human-readable. BEAT THE BOMB has 7 bullets, several of which restate the same scope from different angles.

key_strengths: Real content underneath — the BEAT THE BOMB and 68W achievements are factually anchored, just buried in noise
Sticks to dates and metrics from the consolidated profile (no fabricated employers or numbers)
key_weaknesses: Zero JD-tailoring — same content regardless of target role
Date format 'YYYY-MM – YYYY-MM' reads as raw data rather than a polished display field ('Sep 2024 – Mar 2026' is the convention)
Skills list runs 50+ items in one mixed line; combat-medic terms (CBRN, Hazmat, decontamination) appear alongside help-desk tools with no clustering
Soft skills section has 35+ entries including buzzwords like 'Personnel Retention Strategies' and 'Cost Management Awareness' that no recruiter associates with this profile
BEAT THE BOMB bullet count (7) is one over the cap and adds restatements rather than distinct scope
Projects section has a vague Strategic Simulation entry with no concrete outcome the reader can scan
specific_fixes: Pick a target role and cut every skill that's not within 2 hops of it (combat-medic skills go to military section only)
Group skills into 3-4 themed buckets (Service Desk & Support / Systems & Networking / Tools & Documentation / Soft)
Cut soft-skills section to ≤6 items most relevant to the target role
Display dates as 'Mon YYYY – Mon YYYY' not 'YYYY-MM – YYYY-MM'
Trim BEAT THE BOMB to 5-6 bullets prioritizing JD-aligned scope + impact metrics
tier_definition_match: Matches bad (~3): a kitchen-sink dump with no editorial decisions made. A reader cannot identify the role being applied to or what the candidate uniquely offers for it. This is the worst-case baseline — what shows up when tailoring fully fails.
